title: draba tomentosa subsp. frigida
intro: Draba tomentosa subsp. frigida is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as a type of wild mustard. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Draba tomentosa subsp. frigida include its ability to relieve congestion, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
